title: Postgres CPU > 90% for 5m
question: Postgres CPU is 95% on prod-db-1 — what is the runbook?
actions: Confirm alert and list long-running queries via pg_stat_activity
Cancel queries running longer than 5 minutes
Check missing indexes (seq_scan vs idx_scan)
Fail over to replica with Patroni if CPU stays high
Escalate to DBA on-call if CPU > 90% for 15 minutes
decision_reason: Known runbook with deterministic steps. Tier-1 can act before paging DBA.

title: API 5xx rate > 2% for 4m
question: API 5xx rate is above 2% on checkout — what should I check?
actions: Check recent deployments and correlate with 5xx spike
Tail checkout-api and gateway logs for ERROR patterns
Check database CPU and slow queries
Verify payment and dependency health
Roll back if issue matches recent bad deployment
decision_reason: Customer revenue at risk. Assistant prepares rollback and escalation path.

title: Queue lag > 30s for 5m
question: Queue lag is above 30 seconds — what should I do?
actions: Check worker pod health and restart counts
Compare queue depth to baseline
Review recent worker deploys
Scale workers if within autoscaling limits
Inspect dead-letter queue for poison messages
decision_reason: Often traffic-shaped. Scale and DLQ inspection are safe Tier-1 actions.

title: Auth login failures > 5% for 5m
question: Users cannot log in after deployment — what should I check?
actions: Check auth-service /health and /ready endpoints
Search logs for OIDC and database errors
Validate JWT_SECRET, AUTH_DB_URL, TOKEN_ISSUER
Verify Postgres and Redis connectivity
Roll back deployment if failure rate remains high
decision_reason: Login failures after deploy often need auth team if rollback does not restore service.

title: Disk usage > 85% for 15m
question: Disk usage is above 85% on prod-app-2 — what should I do first?
actions: Identify mount point and largest log directories
Rotate or compress application logs per SOP
Extend volume if rotation is insufficient
Escalate if disk crosses 90% during business hours
decision_reason: Log rotation is a standard Tier-1 action when documented in monitoring reference.

title: Non-prod scrape timeout (noise)
question: Staging metrics scrape is slow — is this actionable?
actions: Confirm environment is non-production
Suppress or snooze if below SLO impact threshold
Log for weekly hygiene review
decision_reason: P4 noise — no customer impact. Safe to suppress after environment check.
